module.title: SAP TM Freight Settlement & Charge Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand freight agreements
learningObjectives.description: Explain contractual pricing terms and their role in TM charge and settlement processes.

learningObjectives.title: Understand rate management
learningObjectives.description: Describe rate determination using agreement components and pricing criteria.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate agreement and rate table
learningObjectives.description: Clarify commercial terms versus pricing values in transportation costing.

sections.content: Freight Agreements define the commercial terms between a shipper and a carrier. They contain transportation rates, pricing conditions, validity periods and service agreements that SAP TM uses during charge calculation and freight settlement. Rate Management ensures transportation costs are calculated consistently according to negotiated contracts.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Freight Agreements answer the question: 'How much should the carrier be paid for this transportation service?'

A carrier signs a one-year contract with a manufacturing company. The Freight Agreement specifies a base transportation rate of ₹40 per kilometer, a fuel surcharge of 8% and a fixed unloading charge. During Freight Settlement, SAP TM automatically applies these rates to every eligible shipment.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain validity dates carefully and review freight agreements regularly to ensure obsolete pricing is not used during charge calculation.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A logistics provider maintained separate Freight Agreements for domestic road transport, international ocean freight and air cargo. SAP TM automatically selected the correct agreement based on transportation mode and route.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Freight Agreements contain commercial terms, while Rate Tables contain the actual pricing values used during charge calculation.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Freight Agreements with Carrier Master Data. Carrier Master Data identifies the transportation provider, whereas Freight Agreements define pricing and contractual conditions.

items: Freight Agreements define carrier pricing contracts.
Freight Agreements support automatic charge calculation.
Validity periods control when pricing is applicable.
Correct agreement maintenance is essential for accurate freight settlement.
